subject: ap-precalculus
title: Sinusoidal Modeling: Tidal Depth & Periodic Height
stimulus: The depth of water at the entrance to a harbor changes sinusoidally with time due to tides. At time $t = 0$ hours, the depth is at the midline value of $8$ meters and decreasing. The amplitude of the tidal variation is $3$ meters, and the period is $12.4$ hours.

parts.prompt:
The depth $D$ of the water in meters at time $t$ hours can be written as $D(t) = a\sin(b(t - c)) + d$.

(i) Find values of $a$, $b$, $c$, and $d$.

(ii) One full cycle of $D$ has five key feature points: the starting midline crossing (decreasing), the minimum, the second midline crossing (increasing), the maximum, and the return to midline (decreasing). Label these points $A$, $B$, $C$, $E$, $F$ respectively. Determine the coordinates $(t, D(t))$ for each of these five points.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), the function is at the midline and decreasing at $t = 0$, so a valid form is $D(t) = -3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) + 8$, giving $a = -3$, $b = \frac{\pi}{6.2}$, $c = 0$, $d = 8$. Accept $a = 3$ with appropriate phase shift $c$ (e.g., $c = -6.2$ shifting right one quarter period). For part (ii), quarter period $= 12.4/4 = 3.1$ hours. Five key points with $t$-coordinates spaced $3.1$ apart: $A = (0, 8)$, $B = (3.1, 5)$, $C = (6.2, 8)$, $E = (9.3, 11)$, $F = (12.4, 8)$. Partial credit if all five $D$-coordinates are correct with incorrect $t$-spacing, or vice versa.

scoring_points.description: Correct parameter values — amplitude $|a| = 3$, midline $d = 8$, $b = \frac{2\pi}{12.4} = \frac{\pi}{6.2}$, and $a$ negative (or positive with appropriate $c$) consistent with a decreasing function at $t = 0$; e.g., $D(t) = -3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) + 8$ with $a = -3$, $b = \frac{\pi}{6.2}$, $c = 0$, $d = 8$

required_elements: phase shift $c$ placing minimum first at $t = 3.1$

wrong_examples: $b = \frac{2\pi}{6.2}$ (using half-period instead of full period in the formula $b = 2\pi/T$)
$a = 3$ with $c = 0$ (positive amplitude with no phase shift gives increasing at $t = 0$, contradicting the given)
common_traps: Period $T = 12.4$ gives $b = \frac{2\pi}{12.4} = \frac{\pi}{6.2}$ — dividing only by $6.2$ (the half-period) is a common error
At $t = 0$ the function is at midline AND decreasing — this requires either $a < 0$ with $c = 0$, or appropriate phase shift

scoring_points.description: Correct coordinates for all five key points with $t$-values spaced $3.1$ hours apart (quarter period): $A = (0, 8)$, $B = (3.1, 5)$, $C = (6.2, 8)$, $E = (9.3, 11)$, $F = (12.4, 8)$
wrong_examples: $t$-values spaced $12.4/5$ apart rather than $12.4/4$ (using 5 equal subdivisions of the period instead of 4)
common_traps: Five key feature points divide one full cycle into four equal quarter-periods ($\Delta t = 12.4/4 = 3.1$)

parts.prompt:
(i) Find all values of $t$ in $0 \leq t < 12.4$ at which the water depth is exactly $6.5$ meters. Express your answers as exact values.

(ii) On the interval $0 < t < 3.1$ (from midline-descending to minimum), describe the sign of $D(t) - 8$ and describe the concavity of the graph of $D$.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), $-3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) + 8 = 6.5 \Rightarrow \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) = \frac{1}{2}$; the reference angle is $\pi/6$, giving $\frac{\pi}{6.2}t = \frac{\pi}{6}$ or $\frac{5\pi}{6}$ in one period. So $t = \frac{6.2}{6} = \frac{31}{30}$ and $t = \frac{6.2 \cdot 5}{6} = \frac{31}{6}$. Both exact values required. For part (ii), $D(t) - 8 = -3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) < 0$ on $(0, 3.1)$ (depth is below midline); the second derivative of $-3\sin(bt)$ is $-3(-b^2)\sin(bt) = 3b^2\sin(bt) > 0$ for $t \in (0, 3.1)$, so the graph is concave up. Both the sign and concavity are required.

scoring_points.description: Both exact values $t = \frac{31}{30}$ and $t = \frac{31}{6}$ with no incorrect values — from $\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) = \frac{1}{2}$, giving $\frac{\pi}{6.2}t = \frac{\pi}{6}$ (so $t = \frac{31}{30}$) or $\frac{\pi}{6.2}t = \frac{5\pi}{6}$ (so $t = \frac{31}{6}$)
wrong_examples: $t = \frac{\pi/6}{\pi/6.2} = 1.033$ and $t = 5.167$ (decimal approximations only — exact values are required)
common_traps: Exact values are required — accept equivalent exact fractions but not decimal-only answers
Sine equals $1/2$ at two distinct angles in $[0, 2\pi)$: $\pi/6$ and $5\pi/6$; both must be converted back to $t$

scoring_points.description: Both descriptions correct — $D(t) - 8 < 0$ on $(0, 3.1)$ (depth is below midline) AND the graph of $D$ is concave up on $(0, 3.1)$

required_elements: graph of $D$ is concave up on $(0, 3.1)$
alternatives.correct_example: $D(t) - 8 = -3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) < 0$ on $(0, 3.1)$ because $\sin > 0$ there; the graph of $D$ is concave up on $(0, 3.1)$ (the function decreases but at a slowing rate toward the minimum).

wrong_examples: '$D(t) - 8 > 0$' (incorrect — depth is below the midline $8$ between midline-descending and minimum)
'concave down' on $(0, 3.1)$ (incorrect — the rate of decrease is slowing as the function approaches the minimum, which is concave up behavior)
common_traps: On $(0, 3.1)$: depth goes from $8$ down to $5$, so it is below the midline — $D - 8 < 0$
Concave up on the descending-to-minimum half: the decrease is decelerating (rate of change becomes less negative), indicating concave up

parts.prompt: A large cargo ship requires a minimum water depth of $9.5$ meters to safely enter the harbor. Determine the total number of hours per tidal cycle (period $= 12.4$ hours) during which the cargo ship can safely enter the harbor. Express your answer as an exact value.
parts.scoring_notes: The safe entry window is $D(t) \geq 9.5$, i.e., $-3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) + 8 \geq 9.5 \Rightarrow \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) \leq -\frac{1}{2}$. In one period $[0, 2\pi]$, sine $\leq -1/2$ on the interval $[7\pi/6, 11\pi/6]$, which spans $4\pi/6 = 2\pi/3$ radians — exactly $1/3$ of a full period. So the ship can enter for $12.4/3 = 62/15$ hours. Exact answer $\frac{62}{15}$ hours required; accept equivalent exact forms.

scoring_points.description: Correct setup — $-3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) + 8 \geq 9.5 \Rightarrow \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) \leq -\frac{1}{2}$; the sine function is $\leq -1/2$ on a sub-interval spanning $\frac{2\pi/3}{2\pi} = \frac{1}{3}$ of a full period (arc from $7\pi/6$ to $11\pi/6$ in the unit circle)

required_elements: the solution spans $1/3$ of the period
alternatives.correct_example: $-3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) + 8 \geq 9.5 \Rightarrow \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) \leq -\frac{1}{2}$; sine $\leq -1/2$ for $\frac{1}{3}$ of a full period

wrong_examples: $D(t) \geq 9.5 \Rightarrow \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) \geq \frac{1}{2}$ (wrong direction of inequality after subtracting and dividing by $-3$)
Stating sine $\leq -1/2$ spans $1/2$ of the period (incorrect — it spans $1/3$)
common_traps: Dividing both sides of $-3\sin(\cdot) \geq 1.5$ by $-3$ reverses the inequality: $\sin(\cdot) \leq -1/2$
From the unit circle: sine $= -1/2$ at $7\pi/6$ and $11\pi/6$; the interval $[7\pi/6, 11\pi/6]$ has length $4\pi/6 = 2\pi/3$, which is $1/3$ of $2\pi$

scoring_points.description: Correct exact total time $\frac{62}{15}$ hours — $\frac{1}{3} \times 12.4 = \frac{12.4}{3} = \frac{62}{15}$ hours; exact form required (accept $\frac{62}{15}$ or equivalent exact fraction, or decimal $\approx 4.133$ hours if derived from exact work)
common_traps: The fraction of the period is $\frac{2\pi/3}{2\pi} = \frac{1}{3}$ — multiply by the full period $12.4$ hours
